content:
Topic sentences have many purposes. A topic sentence: makes a claim – a mini “thesis” statement; explains what the paragraph will say; unifies the sentences of a paragraph; relates the paragraph to the purpose of the paper; supports the claim made in the paper. A topic sentence is usually the first or second sentence of a paragraph. This will help the reader understand the paragraph’s point before reading it. The topic sentence may sometimes be the final sentence – this is used to effectively “wrap up” the paragraph and emphasize a point.

For further reading on Audience: https://writingcenter.unc.edu/tips-and-tools/audience/

Please refer to this worksheet to better understand how to use your sources most effectively:




—————————

Week 4 Discussion Forum

Thank you for participating in the discussion forums, x!

Remember, to receive an exceptional score in all areas of the discussion post rubric, you must post an original, thoughtful response (~250 words minimum) by Wednesday at midnight. This original post should include a critical assessment of the reading material presented with quotes from these sources. You must also respond in a substantial and meaningful way to at least two peer’s posts before the weekends.
